edges.desc: explore the island that has long been a battlefield in the ancient dragon war
use as home base during the adventure, where they can rest and get supplies between visits to other sites
established and leads the cloister of Dragon's Rest, living there in human guise
has been present on Stormwreck Isle for centuries, including killing a blue dragon there
her establishment of Dragon's Rest as a safe haven is where the adventure begins for the party
Runara killed a blue dragon at the Clifftop Observatory site
The characters arrive at locations marked on the map of Stormwreck Isle
wizard casts flaming sphere against fire snake, but the creature is immune to fire damage
The party is traveling by ship at sea near Stormwreck Isle
The merrow is encountered at sea during the party's voyage
The merrow attempts to extort the party, speaking in an intimidating voice
left the bustling port of Neverwinter a few days ago
demands tribute payment of 400 gp or equivalent goods, which can be negotiated down with Charisma checks
players decide their characters will attack the merrow if other approaches fail, and they should be able to defeat it in 1-2 rounds
The characters seek out Runara's wisdom and assistance regarding the dangers facing Stormwreck Isle
The party is rowed ashore to the beach/dock area
The zombies move toward and attack the characters if they choose to fight, fighting until defeated
cleric uses sacred flame cantrip to deal radiant damage to zombies
rescues the characters by defeating the zombies that were attacking them
wake up in the temple (area A5 in Dragon's Rest) after being rescued
The characters escape from the zombies and head up the path toward Dragon's Rest
Elder Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest cloister
Characters can sleep in the monastic cells (area A1)
Characters eat with the community in the dining room (area A3)
Myla sells equipment from the Basic Rules to the characters
Myla is a resident of Dragon's Rest
arrives at the cloister gardens, drawing attention from all residents
Runara maintains a secret lair in a cave accessed by an undersea tunnel, which she carefully enters and leaves without being spotted
The other residents of the cloister think she lives in the temple at the top of the island (area A5)
Nine kobolds live at Dragon's Rest, devoted to Runara
Many bands of kobolds have been drawn to Stormwreck Isle over centuries by draconic magic
Kobolds at Dragon's Rest are utterly devoted to Runara and share her ideals of justice and compassion
Myla was helped by Runara during her recovery and now serves as a kobold tinkerer at Dragon's Rest
Myla's brothers Mek and Minn now follow Sparkrender, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling in Clifftop Observatory
Myla's wings were badly injured in an attack by Stirge, which she describes as 'hungry, icky, blood-sucking, bat-things'
Kobolds at Dragon's Rest are initially friendly toward the adventurers unless otherwise noted
Tarak tends the cloister's garden plots, growing flowers, herbs, and vegetables as the resident botanist
Frequently visits the sea caves to acquire heart cap mushrooms from the myconids that live there
Uses heart cap mushrooms from the sea caves to make Potions of Healing
Initially friendly toward visitors, but becomes indifferent or hostile if they pry into his past about the Gilded Gallows thieves' guild
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation at Dragon's Rest
can shift her attitude to friendly by engaging in conversation on history, ethics, and the impact of individual actions
occupies the second cell from the west
occupies the third cell from the west
occupies the fourth cell, which is cluttered with junk and tools
can occupy the vacant westernmost cell if they don't mind sharing
can operate the winch to pull the pallet back up 10 feet as an action
can pull the lever that locks the winch in place, causing the pallet to fall down to the water
Tarak cooks meals in the dining room on certain days
The cloister residents share three daily meals in the dining room
Everyone's favorite days are when Tarak cooks, though nobody says it out loud
spends nearly half her time in the cloister library studying, copying, and annotating the collection
comes to the cloister library to read and discuss various works
comes to the cloister library to read and discuss various works
visits the cloister library and is described as studious among the kobolds
helps Runara maintain the temple along with other residents of the cloister
when cast in the temple, reveals a faint aura around the statue of Bahamut
zombies are present on the island and cause trouble if not fought initially
three zombies attack Blepp and Tarak in the cloister gardens
a zombie hit Blepp, leaving him with 2 hit points
zombies overpower the unarmed Tarak if characters don't help
defeated the zombies when they first arrived on the island
asks the characters to visit the sea caves, find out what's wrong with the myconids, and bring back heart cap mushrooms
Runara suggests the answer to the shipwreck mystery is likely to be found on the wreck of Compass Rose
Runara is present at the cloister where she can be spoken to
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Tarak
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Varnoth
promises to direct the wizard to the observatory after the characters help deal with other problems on the island
fears Aidron went to the ancient observatory but dares not go herself to avoid reopening old wounds
Runara keeps an eye on the characters and can rescue them if they get into trouble on the island
three fume drakes are the guardians of the hot spring pool, initially hidden in the water
the fume drakes guard the hot spring pool and become hostile if characters gather mushrooms or enter the water
fume drakes emerge from the water to attack the group if they attempt to gather mushrooms or enter the hot spring
The party arrives at and explores the luminescent cove with its hot spring, glowing water, and colorful mushrooms
can spend 10 minutes bathing in the waters to regain hit points once per day
hostile toward the characters, views them as intruders in its territory, goal is to drive them away
was stranded here after the ship carrying its performing troupe crashed on the northern rocks
encounter is particularly appropriate if characters are traveling across the island rather than following the coast
Four kobolds are hiding in rocks and light foliage around Dragon's Rest, attempting to ambush travelers
One winged kobold participates in the ambush around Dragon's Rest
The kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara
The winged kobold rejects the peaceful teachings of Runara
The kobolds attempt to ambush and attack the characters traveling around Dragon's Rest
The winged kobold participates in the ambush against the characters
was formed from magic-fueled volcanic activity in the tomb of the red dragon Sharruth
suggests that all is not well beneath Stormwreck Isle through its activity
could investigate Sharruth's tomb through winding tunnels deep into the earth
The characters are assumed to come to the Seagrow Caves location
The characters are assumed to go to the wreck of the Compass Rose after the caves
located in sea caves on the southwest side of Stormwreck Isle
from Dragon's Rest
used to trade with the myconids, exchanging fungi for food scraps
recently rejected Tarak's visits and placed a guardian to keep visitors away
leader of the myconid colony
noxious fumes from the dragon's tomb are poisoning the myconids and twisting their gardens
fumes normally filter up through rock and vent through a cavern at the back of Seagrow Caves
The dark gray basalt walls of the caves originate from Sharruth's undersea tomb
The lesser restoration spell can end the poisoned condition caused by toxic fumes in the caves
Dragon's Rest has a rowboat the characters can take
Tarak prefers the boat method for reaching Seagrow Caves
The characters arrive at Seagrow Caves at sea level
The party is positioned at the cliffside plaza looking down at the opening below
can wait for low tide or row/swim into the tunnel at high tide
can access via the flooded tunnel from B1 at high tide
Myconid adults live in Seagrow Caves and use Rapport Spores to warn visitors to leave
Myconid sprouts live in Seagrow Caves and flee to warn adults of intruders
Sprouts flee toward the nearest adults to warn them of intruders
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids, and sprouts know she has fallen ill
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids, and adults know she fell ill after investigating the crystal cave
Mentioning Tarak's name or presenting his offering grants advantage on Charisma checks with myconids
If the characters attack, the myconids defend themselves
The characters are present in the myconid colony where they are communicating telepathically with the myconids via Rapport Spores
The party is present in location b3
The party is present in location b4
lurks in the water of the tunnel at area B1
attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, including the party
created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away from the seagrow caves
The party is present at the entrance of the cave where multicolored fungus covers the walls and glowing spores swirl in the water
two Stirge clinging to the tunnel ceiling in this area
join the battle if fighting breaks out in this area
Molen and Kraz are spreading fertilizer from area B3
Hipsiz and Rugoso tend the mushrooms near the upper pool in area B2
three Violet Fungus grow among the harmless mushrooms in the cave
if characters move into the cave toward the myconids, one violet fungus attacks them with tendrils that cause immediate rot
characters kill the violet fungi using ranged attacks while staying out of their reach
The Myconid Sprout avoids the characters and the violet fungi
The Myconid Adult moves quickly to protect the sprouts when aware of danger through combat noise or Distress Spores
After characters defeat violet fungi, the adults agree to accompany and vouch for them with the rest of the colony
The adults vouch for characters with the colony, improving other myconids' attitudes to indifferent
can be created from mushrooms found in the fungus farm
gathers fertilizer for use in area B2
six Stirge emerge from the nest in the chamber
the sprouts flee toward area B4 at the first sign of intruders
relies on the distraction of the stirges to escape from intruders
Six myconid adults are present in this location, with two tending to four others in a meld trance
tending to the unconscious leader and zealously defending them from intruders
will make an elixir of health for the characters if they bring back a ruby morel fungus
Two Fume Drakes lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in the cave
The presence of the red dragon Sharruth deep beneath the island causes volcanic activity that produces the noxious fumes in the cave
released from the broken crystal and immediately attacks the characters, seeing them only as fuel
emerges from the broken crystal in the western fissure cave
Sinensa is the myconid leader in the Seagrow Caves
Sinensa gives the characters the ruby morel and permission to keep treasure if they destroy the orange crystal and are present when Sinensa awakens
Tarak creates the elixir of health using the ruby morel
The characters are ready to visit Clifftop Observatory after reaching 3rd level
Residents of Dragon's Rest urge the characters to visit the wreck of Compass Rose next
settled into the wreck of Compass Rose
feasts on sailors, with survivors becoming zombies
lures ships to crash on the shoal around the island
shambling zombies come ashore from shipwrecks
menaces the people of Dragon's Rest
some zombies have been trapped in the wreck for years
can be found aboard sunken ships like Compass Rose
can be found aboard sunken ships like Compass Rose
The characters can take the rowboat to travel to the wreck of Compass Rose
The characters can tie up the rowboat to the south (starboard) side of the wreck
The characters can climb onto the main deck at area C1 after tying up the rowboat
The characters can swim through the hole in the hold at area C9 to get aboard
topped with a crow's nest overflowing with debris
climb the rope ladder up the mast to reach the crow's nest
has made the crow's nest its lair and nest
the harpy is out looking for another ship to lure onto the rocks near the wreck
two small tiger eye gems found in the crow's nest during search
one bloodstone gem found in the crow's nest during search
characters are searching through the crow's nest
hears noise from C3 and repeatedly crashes against the door to C4
two zombies are inside the captain's quarters, either beside the door or shuffling around the cabin
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
The fine clothes are depicted being worn by Brastos in the portrait located at this location
rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard at location c6
is an obvious threat in the location
lurks in the aft part of the hold, waiting to attack
is more cunning than the zombies and uses them as a distraction
waits to attack until characters are busy with the zombie, hoping to paralyze and drag one away
The characters are present at location c8 where the encounter occurs
characters can use a crowbar to pry open crates more quickly
a 20-pound sack of whole cloves can be found in one of the crates
a fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay can be found in one of the crates
a spell scroll sealed in a leather case can be found in one of the crates
the spell scroll contains the command spell
venturing into the submerged hold at location C9
contained within the iron chest on the floor of the hold
three turquoise gems are inside the pouch
contained within the iron chest on the floor of the hold
can be cast by characters to reveal the talisman's necromantic magic
The narrator secures Aleitha's talisman with a book in a chest at the wreck site
Many Undead creatures like Ghoul worship Orcus, the Demon Prince of Undeath
lands on the main deck (c1) after the characters hear a heavy thump
confronts the characters on the main deck and uses Luring Song to charm them
The harpy's treasure is stored in the crow's nest, and it might leave if the treasure is returned
Characters can reduce the harpy to fewer than half its hit points, potentially causing it to flee
Characters can negotiate with the harpy, convincing it to leave in exchange for returning its treasure from the crow's nest
The characters are present at location C2 where the encounter occurs
experiences a divine vision where sunlight breaks through darkness over the island and feels the love and approval of their god
If necessary, you can have Runara warn the characters that they're not ready to face this part of the adventure yet
battled centuries ago, with Sparkrender being a descendant of Runara's ancient rival
Sparkrender has made the observatory ruins his home and seeks to claim the destructive magic there
Aidron left the safety of Dragon's Rest where he was under Runara's tutelage
Aidron came to the ancient observatory and is now imprisoned there by Sparkrender
Sparkrender overpowered Aidron and imprisoned him in the observatory ruins
Sparkrender attempted to turn Aidron against Runara and now plans a ritual that will cost Aidron's life
The characters approach the observatory by land, seeing Sparkrender fly by and examining crystalline protrusions
Assuming the characters continue toward the observatory, they soon arrive at area D1
can row to the observatory as an alternative approach
can climb bluffs from beached boat to reach area D1
can climb to area D2 from boat tied at pillar base
can climb to area D3 from boat tied at pillar base
can climb to area D4 from boat tied at pillar base
can climb to area D5 from boat tied at pillar base
can explore the rotunda ruins where the sculpture is located
can explore the observatory tower where Sparkrender and another dragon argued
can explore the observatory tower where the other dragon is currently located
can explore area D3 where the caved-in wall is visible from
two winged kobolds are locked in battle with eight stirges
kobolds will help characters if characters help them fight stirges
Mek and Minn are brothers of Myla but left her for dead after stirge attack
if friendly, kobolds offer to help retrieve moonstone key from area D1
The party is exploring the Forgotten Realms world where the astronomical sculpture is located
can create a bridge back to the overlook using the moonstone key in the western dragon statues
can create a bridge to the crumbled and isolated tower using the moonstone key in the southwestern dragon statues
Three kobolds named Ekrash, Erp, and Hev currently inhabit this camp
Two winged kobolds named Nuhro and Snirke currently inhabit this camp
Kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
Winged kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
can be used by a character to cross the gap and explore the isolated study alone
the moonstone key retrieved from area D1 can be used again in area D2
found together in the hidden compartment behind the loose brick
the secret library below (area D6) where Bronze Dragon Wyrmling is imprisoned
the characters can free Bronze Dragon Wyrmling and fight Sparkrender with Aidron's help
the characters can waken the dragon and fight him now
can potentially wake and fight the dragon if stealth checks fail
Sparkrender becomes hostile and attacks if a character mentions Runara
five pale blue quartz crystals are part of Sparkrender's hoard at the clifftop observatory
five blue jasper stones are part of Sparkrender's hoard at the clifftop observatory
Sparkrender forced Aidron into the library and caused a wall to cave in, sealing the exit
Aidron asks for help defeating Sparkrender and expresses concern for the island's inhabitants
If characters haven't defeated Sparkrender, Aidron flies to the top of the observatory tower to confront Sparkrender
Characters can clear rubble to access the secret library where Aidron is trapped
Characters must climb down from area D5 to access the rubble blocking the library entrance
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
the spell is contained on the 2nd level spell scroll
the spell reveals the location of the battleaxe without requiring an ability check
the spell reveals the location of the spell scroll without requiring an ability check
Aidron (bronze dragon wyrmling) is imprisoned in area D6
Sparkrender's behavior in this encounter is described in 'Waking the Dragon' in area D5
Runara gives each character a potion of healing as a reward
Runara gives each character an exquisite pearl worth 100 gp as a reward
